claim_short: 1812.10669 Completeness in the Mackey topology by norming subspaces. Problem 4.15 asks whether X* is fully Mackey complete whenever X is weak-star sequentially dense in X**. Partial positive theorem: if 1<p<infty and X=(oplus_{gamma in Gamma} E_gamma)_{ell_p}, where each E_gamma is separable and contains no ell_1, then X is weak-star sequentially dense in X** and X* is fully Mackey complete. The proof gives a...
notes: registry_status=partial | review_focus=Please check the countable-support localization lemma, especially the passage from a norming subspace \(Y\subset X^{**}\) to a countable \(A\) for which \(Y\cap X_A^{**}\) remains norming for \(X_A^*\). This is the core new argument.
packet_title: Partial solution packet: an ell_p-sum positive class for Problem 4.15
source_authors: A. J. Guirao, G. Martínez-Cervantes, J. Rodríguez
source_question: Source: A. J. Guirao, G. Martinez-Cervantes, and J. Rodriguez, "Completeness in the Mackey topology by norming subspaces", arXiv:1812.10669 / J. Math. Anal. Appl. 478 (2019), 776--789.
source_title: Completeness in the Mackey topology by norming subspaces
